what_this_pass_proved: Tightened tolerances (Tier 1: 12°, Tier 2: 9°, Tier 3: 8°→6° smooth ramp) make placement feel precise without being punishing — the old 15° at Tier 1 was too generous to teach anything.
Coordinate prompts delayed to Tier 3 only (Tier 2 uses degrees + radians) prevents a difficulty spike when coords appear — radians are comfortable before coords are introduced.
Within-tier round shuffling per session prevents sequence memorization — the same tier feels different each play.
Warm-up bonus (+3s on first round of each tier) prevents the 'surprise new difficulty' feeling at tier transitions.
Close threshold tightened from 2.5× to 2× tolerance makes near-miss feedback more honest — fewer false 'close' results.
open_questions: Is the Tier 3 ramp (8°→7°→7°→6°→6°) smooth enough, or does the 7→6 step feel like a spike on the last two rounds?
Does the warm-up bonus feel like a natural breathing room or like inconsistency in the timer?
With coords delayed to Tier 3, do players feel unprepared when they first encounter (−½, √3/2) at round 11?

bugs_fixed.description: Tier 3 tolerance had a spike: 8/8/8/8/5 — the last round dropped 3° suddenly. Smoothed to 8/7/7/6/6.
Tier 2 round 6 used coordinate prompt (−½, √3/2) before radians were comfortable. Changed to radians 2π/3.
Tier 1 tolerance at 15° was too generous — players could place roughly and still get 'correct'. Tightened to 12°.

known_limits: Tolerance ramp has not been validated against real play data. The 12°→6° range is a design hypothesis.
Round shuffling is within-tier only. Cross-tier order is fixed (always Tier 1→2→3). May feel predictable after multiple sessions.
Warm-up bonus is a flat +3s. May need to vary by tier if Tier 3 warm-up needs more time.

auditor_results.notes: Tolerance ramp [12, 12, 12, 12, 12, 9, 9, 9, 9, 9, 8, 7, 7, 6, 6] — smooth within each tier. Timer ramp [18, 18, 18, 18, 18, 14, 14, 14, 14, 14, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10] — stepped by tier with warm-up bonus on transitions.

next_pass_first_obligation: Observe whether the Tier 2→3 transition (radians to coordinates) feels fair with the warm-up bonus, or whether coordinates need their own mini-tutorial before the first coord round.
